summary:
The primary characteristic property of an impression compound relates to its high flow when initially mixed and handled, making option B a suitable choice based on immediate post-mix properties that are crucial clinically. \(\blacksquare\) To summarize Agent2’s reasoning:

Impression compounds have excellent flow characteristics right after they're mixed; this makes them easy to work with quickly without distorting. Therefore, \( \textbf{\highflowproperty}\) fits as the most critical feature from the listed options. 

This summary captures both core aspects—immediate mixability and practical application—in just two concise statements aligned closely with clinical relevance.
